title: Programs
creator: digoadmin
content:
1. Rethinking Development

The Rethinking Development focus area of DBI works on research and advocacy to bring forward concepts, frameworks, policies, processes, technologies and practical activities that promote ecological sustainability, economic justice, and social prosperity.

2. Climate Justice

Solving the climate crisis requires a systemic transformation of our society and economy. DBI promotes real solutions and alternatives based on the ideas and experience from communities at the forefront of the climate change. Our work on climate justice program involves policy analysis, advocacy, outreach and networking with like-minded organizations and individuals at the national and international level.

3. Sustainability Watch

The vision of modernization and development propagated and implemented by political, bureaucratic and technocratic establishment impact people’s livelihood and environment. As the country has entered into new federal governance system, a number of environmental regulatory provisions are being revised or amended. In this context, DBI’s work around sustainability watch the monitor and track the enforcement of environmental regulatory regime and progress (or the lack) of policies and plans related to sustainable development in Nepal.

4. Sustainability Education

Through sustainability education, DBI provides space for creation and sharing of knowledge that incorporates issues of ecological sustainability and social equity. Through awareness, education, and training, DBI helps people understand that environment and development are inseparable and that ecological devastation brings inequality and injustice.

title: Introduction
creator: digoadmin
content:
Digo Bikas Institute (DBI) is a research and advocacy organization committed to promote ecological sustainability and social equity at policy and community level. Based in Kathmandu, DBI believes that Nepal (and the rest of the world) can develop meaningfully by identifying and applying alternative ways of meeting human needs without harming the nature and fellow human beings. Vision Digo Bikas Institute envisions an ecologically sound, socially just and prosperous society, and that it will continue to be so for future generations. Mission Digo Bikas Institute’s mission is to undertake research and action to stimulate informed discussion and strategic thought to advance alternative and local solutions to global  problems. Values
  • Equity
  • Fairness and Justice
  • Integrity
  • Transparency and accountability
  • Inclusiveness
Our Approach Digo Bikas Institute carries out research, training and advocacy on all aspects of sustainable development. Our research work aims to build evidence for bringing positive policy change. We empower and engage critical mass of informed citizens through our education and trainings. We conduct advocacy work based on the evidence generated through our research and analysis by involving the informed citizens.
We use different approaches to achieve our vision and mission those include research and analysis, seminars and conferences, education and training programs, correspondence and fact finding missions, public consultations and legal interventions, network building and international solidarity, joint campaigns and media.

title: Jobs and Internships
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_single_image image="1927" img_size="large"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_column_text]

APPLY FOR

RESEARCH GRANT PROPOSAL 2026

ABOUT DIGO BIKAS INSTITUTE Digo Bikas Institute (DBI) is a research and advocacy organisation committed to promoting ecological sustainability and social equity at both policy and community levels. DBI believes that Nepal and the world can develop meaningfully by identifying and applying alternative ways of meeting human needs without harming nature or fellow human beings. DBI’s vision is of an ecologically sound, socially just, and prosperous society that endures for future generations. Its mission is to undertake research and action that stimulates informed discussion and advances alternative, locally rooted solutions to global problems. PURPOSE OF THE GRANT The DBI Research Grant is established to support former DBI fellows, trainees, and programme participants in conducting rigorous, mission-aligned research. The grant seeks to build a thriving community of scholar-practitioners who continue to advance DBI’s principles beyond their formal association with the Institute. By investing in its alumni, DBI aims to expand the evidence base for ecological sustainability and social equity, foster peer learning, and amplify local voices in national and global policy discussions. GRANT AT A GLANCE
Funder Digo Bikas Institute (DBI)
Grant Amount NPR 30,000 per research
Total Number of Grants 3 (2 for DBI alumni + 1 will be open to all)
Application Rolling basis
Review Review by the DBI Grant Committee
Institutional Affiliation Applicants may be affiliated with academia, civil society, government, or the private sector, or may be independent researchers.
Research Alignment Proposed research must clearly align with DBI’s principles
Eligibility DBI alumni as well as who meet DBI’s principles
No Active DBI Employment Current full-time DBI staff are not eligible.
Ethics  & Integrity Applicants must adhere to ethical research standards and DBI’s values of integrity, transparency, and inclusiveness.
Grant Duration Up to 3 months per project
Submission Mode Online portal / KoboToolbox
THEMATIC AREAS OF FOCUS Proposals may address any research question that is substantively grounded in one or more of DBI’s core thematic areas. Interdisciplinary and cross-cutting research is strongly encouraged. Proposals on emerging or cross-cutting themes are welcome, provided they demonstrate clear alignment with DBI’s vision and mission. HOW TO APPLY Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year. Proposals received before the 15th of any month will be considered in the immediately following quarterly review cycle.  Application Link: https://ee.kobotoolbox.org/single/CfSj97Yg Required Documents:
  • Digo Bikas Institute (Research Grant Form) (link available above)
  • Research Proposal (maximum 10 pages, excluding annexes) (see outline below)
  • Curriculum Vitae
  • A cover letter
Research Proposal Outline:
  1. Title and Abstract (max 300 words)
  2. Problem Statement and Research Rationale
  3. Research Objectives and Questions
  4. Methodology and Research Design
  5. Alignment with DBI Principles and Thematic Areas
  6. Expected Outputs, Outcomes, and Impact
  7. Dissemination and Advocacy Plan
  8. Workplan and Timeline
  9. Ethical Considerations
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for a presentation, and final selections will be made based on a competitive evaluation process. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Support the Organization
creator: digoadmin
content: Digo Bikas Institute needs your support. Your small contribution can make a big difference. We assure you that the money will be used responsibly. You can also specify if would like your contribution to be used specifically for a certain activity. We will keep you updated on our activities. If you like to support our work, Contact Us Your contribution will help us: – Carry out analysis and research on providing local solutions to global problems and leapfrogging towards a development model that is ecologically sound and socially just. – Develop ideas for a sustainable world free from climate chaos. – Train and educate people on different aspects of sustainability. – Monitor whether development projects in Nepal comply environmental safeguards.

title: Avinash Jha
creator: bibek
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="550" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Avinash Jha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Avinash leads a parallel career as a community organizer and an engineer. He has more than eight years of experience in Community Leadership. As an Education Director of the education non-profit, United World Schools Nepal, he has been leading education development across 35 community-based schools in rural Nepal. In prior engineering career, Avinash has supervised the implementation thousand plus projects on Solar Home Systems, Solar Water Pumping for irrigation, Micro-Hydro, and Improved Cooking Stoves in rural communities of Nepal. Along with his friends, Avinash also developed an award-winning mobile application that helps Nepal’s farming communities. In past, he has been awarded as a winner of Ncell App Camp 2014, Rec@nnect Challenge 2014, and ICT for mountain development award by ICIMOD in 2014. Furthermore, he does not shy away from volunteering his free time for any inspirational opportunity that comes his way.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Bijaya Subba
creator: bibek
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="553" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Bijaya Subba

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Bijaya Subba has long engaged in broader social issues for marginalized communities and has grounded experience of working for identity, language, justice advocacy with rural communities in different parts of Nepal.  She has worked in the Chepang community and in Karnali for decades, in issues of holistic wellbeing and later worked on formal education recognizing it to be a medium for systemic reform. She is always open to ideas and especially puts effort to ensure engagement and participation of the marginalized in all settings. At present, she is an Operations Manager at the Centre for Educational Policies and Practices (CEPP) through which she works to improve public schools through policy reforms at different levels and ground team mobilization. She previously worked with Karnali livelihoods and empowerment and women's reproductive rights and Education for All Chepang on education, indigenous and reproductive issues. She is also organized many training, workshops, and seminars for school management committees, child-friendly teachers, education activists, life skills, Psychosocial and rights-based approaches to development.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Dristy Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="792" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Dristy Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Dristy Shrestha is research officer for livable cities program at Digo Bikas Institute. She holds a Master’s degree in Sustainable Development from Kathmandu University and Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science from Tribhuwan University. She received Master’s degree research Grant from The Mountain Institute for her study entitled “Identifying potential sources for sustaining Ecosystem-based Adaptation in Chilime watershed”.   She has been engaged in environment and cycle activism from 2012, and been part of the vehicle free movement campaign in Kathmandu valley since 2014.  She has been part of various campaigns on climate change, air pollution, bicycle activism and vehicle free development. She has a core interest to work in local sustainability planning, indigenous communities, climate change awareness and local economics.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Nirakar Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="796" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Nirakar Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Nirakar is an ambitious and ardent "student" of Architecture, urged to connect People with earth and narrates the story to enhance the content and believes that the true essence of architecture lies in its execution and user experience. Understanding the deeper co-relation between built environment, human and the nature, he is an enthusiastic learner and advocates regionalism, sustainability and vernacular architecture. With an aim to promote vernacular Architecture and sustainability. He is Province no.1. Coordinator for Liveable cities Program at Digo Bikas Institute.  He holds Bachelors in Architecture at Purwanchal campus, Dharan, affiliated to Tribhuwan University. He coordinated architecture exhibition-2076 at Purwanchal campus, Dharan with theme of "Chautari" advocating and promoting the essence of open public community spaces. He has conducted research on "Taadey Ghar- colonnaded structures of Terai region of Nepal" through a vernacular and regionalism approach to promote sustainability.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Dikshya Singh Rathour

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Dikshya holds a Bachelor degree in Development Studies from Kathmandu University and an enthusiastic graduate in International Relations from the University of Warsaw, Poland. An activist/feminist presently employed at Women for Human Rights (WHR) leading the WHR CPS project team and have a strong knowledge of the peace-building sector in a post-conflict country. She is an entrepreneur at Freesia.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Richa Palikhe
creator: bibek
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="555" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Richa Palikhe

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Reecha has been an entrepreneur for nearly a decade. She is a co-founder of PAGE Pvt. Ltd. - a successful promotional supplies company based in Kathmandu. She has also co-founded Make Your Mark Pvt. Ltd.- a company that makes 'Statement Apparels' empowering the customers to express their beliefs and sentiments through their outfit.  Reecha prefers to call herself a new-age entrepreneur. She believes the true Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) is not how a company spends some of its money while helping the community, but how a company earns all of its money while serving the community (its customers and other stakeholders).  Reecha thinks given the grave environmental challenges and the stark social inequalities of the day, all new age entrepreneurs must pursue social goals alongside profit. She has immense interest in promoting the sustainable ways of life. Reecha sits on the Board of Directors of DBI to help the non-profit in whatever way she could, and to learn newer ways to enhance sustainable practices within her own businesses.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Sabin Rimal
creator: bibek
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="556" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Sabin Rimal

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Sabin Rimal holds a Masters Degree in Sociology. Currently, he is working as Program Manager at United World Schools, where he is responsible for carrying out the activities under the United World Schools Nepal Teaching Fellowship Program. He is also an educator; he is working as a lecturer of Social Work at the Everest Innovative College. Before working at United World Schools, he has worked with organizations like YUWA, Freedom Forum, and Nepal Music Festival.    He has conducted training sessions on Program Management, Proposal Development, Team Building, Leadership, Advocacy, and Campaign design to young people of different colleges and youth groups under multiple projects. He was awarded as YUWA Staff of the year for his contributions to YUWA as a Program Director. His area of expertise is in designing training, designing programs, designing events, designing fundraising activities, and designing campaigns. He has supported different national and international organizations like YUWA, United Nations Peace Fund Nepal (UNPFN), Search For Common Ground (SFCG), All Associations of INGO, GIZ, Data4Development, and Accountability Lab to conduct short term programs working in different positions.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Saunak Bhatta
creator: bibek
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="557" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Saunak Bhatta

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Believer in the power of Creativity, Leadership, Passionate living and Sustainable Education, Saunak Bhatta, is a dynamic motivational speaker, campaigner, personal development coach and trainer whose Motivational seminars have turned out to be Motivational concerts. His programs have directly touched over forty-three thousand lives at National and International level. His sessions have raised many leaders, entrepreneurs, organisations and also helped in the recovery of several suicidal attempts. He uses the tools and techniques of Hypnosis Therapy, Neuro-Linguistic Programming, Experiential Education, Appreciative Inquiry, Motivational Psychology and the approaches of Personal Coaching, Stage Motivation, camps and training to create significant impact.  He is the Executive Director of We Inspire Nepal (WIN), through which he has been introducing an inspirational culture throughout the nation. His motivational portfolio and training experiences have outreached over eight countries across Asia and Europe. Along with the conduction of biggest motivational seminar of Nepal and many other houseful shows, his inspiring words has been spread among National Cricket Team of Nepal, Political groups, Corporate leaders, Students, Principals, Psychologically vulnerable communities, Educational Leaders and other diverse stakeholders.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Shreni Rajbhandary
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="624" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Shreni Rajbhandary

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Shreni is an environment enthusiast who has an undergraduate degree in Development Studies. She has been involved in climate change activism and advocacy for the past six years. Shreni is keen on learning about how the development interventions create a lasting impact on the communities. She has four years of experience in governance, energy, climate change, and connectivity. She also has working experience as an Urban Planning Officer for the Integrated Urban Development Plan of 14 municipalities in Nepal. She has research experiences with prestigious institutions such as the Asian Institute of Technology (Department of Water Engineering and Management), Yale University, Governance Lab, Nepal-Japan Project, Durham University etc. Moreover, she is a U.S Embassy Youth Council alumni. She is also a prominent speaker who spoken in BBC thrice about meritocracy and climate justice. She has an excellent volunteerism spirit with volunteering experiences since the age of 12 in many other notable organisations.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Arati Poudel
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="625" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Arati Poudel

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Arati Poudel is a climate researcher with a special interest in climatic extremes. She did her master's by research in physical geography with a specialization in hydro-meteorology from the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences. She has published a scientific paper on the temperature extremes of Nepal in the International Journal of Climatology. While pursuing her undergraduate in Environment science she was involved in youth-led climate activism, climate policy research, and consultations since 2013. Before joining DBI, Arati worked as a Researcher at Kathmandu center for research and education (KCRE), CAS-TU. She sees herself working as a climate scientist in the future. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Rijan Lal Mulmi
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="626" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Rijan Lal Mulmi

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Rijan Lal Mulmi:  With his academic background in Environmental Management and professional background as a researcher, he will be researching on the topic, “Community Solar Micro-grid”. He has had previous experience in research with Community Solar Micro-grid in Okhaldhunga. Rijan Lal Mulmi has been an environmental activist for a while. He was been a part of various national and international conferences relating to climate change. He also has taken part in and organized events relating to climate change in Nepal. He holds bachelor's degree in Environment Management from Pokhara University, Nepal. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Abhishek Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="702" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Abhishek Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Abhishek Shrestha is a Co-founder of Digo Bikas Institute and leads Climate justice Program at Institute. He is a Chevening Scholar who graduated in Masters in Climate Change and policy from the University of Sussex,  UK in 2013 and BSc. Environmental Science from Kathmandu University in 2008.

He received the  “Climate Change Award” in 2017 from the Government of Nepal for his work on Climate Change Policy and Advocacy. He has decade-long experience working as an activist, researcher, campaigner, policy analyst, and strategic communicator at a national and international level on climate change and environmental governance. He has been awarded  “JWH Initiative Environment leadership Award 2017. 

He follows UN Climate Negotiation closely and has been participating since 2009, COP 15, and attended 8 COP meetings until COP 25 as a Youth representative, CSO representative, and Government delegation. He has been  working with diverse national and international climate justice groups since 2008. He has worked as Contributing Author for Government of Nepal publication on “ Key Issues for Nepal at different UN Climate Conference meetings and member of Government delegation at UN Climate Conference in Bonn 2017 (COP 23).

Abhishek has travelled to over 20 countries in Asia, Europe, Africa, South America, and North America and was invited to speak at different national and international conferences on the issue of climate change, climate activism, community solutions, and movement building. He was elected to Serve as YOUNGO Focal Point from Global South for 2012-2013 and interim focal point in COP-17, Durban (YOUNGO- Youth constituency at UNFCCC to ensure positive and constructive participation of the international youth in the UNFCCC) and is a British Council International Climate Champion 2010. He is the Coordinator of Nepal Environment Society- Province 1 and General Secretary of Chevening Alumni Association Nepal. He follows international and Nepal Cricket Closely. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Sagun Timilsina
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="673" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Sagun Timilsina

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Sagun is a  young enthusiast learner in the field of Finance, currently pursuing Bachelors of Business Studies from TU. She is working as Admin and Finance executive at Digo Bikas Institute.  She had worked as Non Gazetted second class officer in District Court Banke for 2 years. She is a good learner and well informed about teamwork to achieve organizational goals, she is an advocate of gender justice, equality, and right-based approach and well versed on the concept of sustainable development. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Shail Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="674" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Shail Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Shail Shrestha is a Co-founder of Digo Bikas Institute and leads Sustainability School Program at Digo Bikas Institute. He began his engagement as a social activist as a bicycle activist while he was studying the interconnections of media, culture, and society as part of his Bachelors in Media Studies at Kathmandu University. He became part of building the Kathmandu cycle city 2020 campaign in 2009, and later became the founding president of Cycle City Network Nepal, which promotes cycling culture across Nepal. Understanding the deeper co-relations between economy, urban development and sustainability, he became founding member of Digo Bikas Institute in 2015. Engaged in broader discourse on urban development and sustainability, he has been advocating about the relevance of the ancient urban settlements of Kathmandu Valley as a solution to Global sustainability crisis. He participated in the global assembly of bicycle activist in World Bicycle Forum 2018, and 2019 representing Nepal and was successful to virtually organize the international event in Nepal in 2020.  He is also the founding member and Nepal coordinator of Carfree Cities Alliance, a network dedicated to the development and promotion of Carfree Urban Future. He believes in the localization of the economy and has been exploring the interconnections of economic systems and sustainability. He completed his Master of Arts in Distance Education, from IGNOU, and has also been involved in the alumni to promote distance education as a sustainable and accessible approach for education.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Alok Rai
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="675" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Alok Rai

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Alok is a graduate from Tribhuvan University, currently pursuing his Chartered Accountancy Courses (CAP – III), and has been working as Finance consultant at Digo Bikas Institute. He has more than 8 years of cumulative experience in the field of Accounts, Audits, Financial and Management Consultancy, and Taxation matters. He has worked in the financial and administrative management of several development organizations for over 5 years to date. His key field of expertise lies in the design and implementation of the internal control system, risk assessment, financial audits, and reporting[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Kshitiz Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="689" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Kshitiz Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Kshitiz is a Photographer by Profession. He holds a degree of Media Studies from Kathmandu University. He has an experience of more than a decade in the field of visual media. With numerous Award winning films/documentary he runs a visual media company called Chhaproma Studios.

Currently he also runs a workshop that trains people on up-cycle used wood and metal. Artist form heart, he is now in journey of endeavouring knowledge about things that is necessary to have sustainable life. He is recently learning and teaching skills like Carpentry, Pottery, Metal work, Cooking and Farming.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text][/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Susmita Mishra
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="693" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Susmita Mishra

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Susmita Mishra holds a Master's degree in Development Studies from Kathmandu University. She has profoundly been active in Volunteerism and climate activism since 2014. She is currently the Secretariat Lead as well as the Regional Coordinator for North, Central and South Asia under Global Affairs Unit for COY 16. She firmly believes to inspire and empower youths to be a part of a bigger picture in the field of Climate action.  She has led and driven wide  youth teams in multiple programs.  She has gained her professional experience as a Project Officer for a local NGO and is skilled in project coordination, communication and linkage building for organizational development as well as youth empowerment and social accountability. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text][/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Raju Tamang Hyolmo Glan
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="710" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Raju Tamang

Hyolmo Glan

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Raju is working in the capacity of the community mobilizer in Dhapsung, Helambu Rural Municipality. He has been involved with DBI since 2016  as a volunteer. At present he ensures timely and regular communication with the community members and supports ground work throughout the year. He provides logistical support and needed assistance to the Dhapsung Mother’s group.  He farms to produce supplies for the family half the year, and is engaged in multiple civil groups like Nepal Foundation, Shakti Samuha as volunteer.
[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Aarati Poudel
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="857" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Aarati Poudel

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]An environmentalist with a strong background in climate science research and policy advocacy, Arati serves as a senior research officer at the Climate Justice Program of the Digo Bikas Institute (DBI). In this role, she oversees policy research, advocacy efforts, and grassroots actions on cross-cutting climate change issues. Her commitment to climate issues spans a decade, beginning with her selection as a City Environment Volunteer for the Kathmandu Metropolitan City. Arati has published peer-reviewed papers on the extreme climatology of Nepal and presented research on the "Role of Women-Led Cooperatives in Climate Finance and Building Climate Resiliency in Nepal" at COP 27 in Sharm el-Sheikh, Egypt. She earned an International Student Scholarship from the University of Chinese Academy of Sciences to pursue her master's degree in research with a specialization in extreme climatology. She has previously worked as a research associate at the Kathmandu Center for Research and Education (KCRE) and was awarded the Digo Bikas Research Fellowship in 2020. Her passion for climate issues and research is matched by her dedication to positively impacting the community through her work. In her free time, she enjoys trekking, traveling and learning about local culture and history.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: CALL FOR APPLICATION:  Climate Justice Summer School 2022
creator: digoadmin
content:
Call for Application: Climate Justice Summer School 2022

The first residential Climate Justice Summer school will be held from September 15-18, 2022 in Dhulikhel and  Kathmandu. Digo Bikas Institute aims to bring together diverse young people from all over Nepal in order to develop a common understanding on climate justice and different facets of the climate crisis. The aim of the summer school is to engage young people aged 18-24 and build a platform to share, learn, and unlearn, climate science, climate policy to politics, organizing, research, policy analysis, grassroots solutions, and UN Climate negotiation.The First cohort of Climate Justice Summer School will select and engage 10-12 youth from all over across Nepal.

There is no application fee for summer school, everything will be covered including Travel for the selected participants.

Deadline for the application: 8th September 2022

 

Please apply here: Application Form

title: Green City Volunteers
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
To implement Nepal's Climate Change Policy-2076 and the country's 2nd Nationally Determined Contribution at the local level, Biratnagar Metropolitan City and Digo Bikas Institute have developed a Green City Volunteer Program (GCV). 38 Youths aged 15-22 (one male and one female from each ward), to ensure ethnic diversity, were selected for GCV.
GCV receives training,  research engagement, an exposure visit, and a community project on 5 themes

Climate Change  |  Solid Waste Management  |  Air Pollution  |  Disaster Risk Reduction  |  Green and Public Spaces

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_single_image image="1559" img_size="large" onclick="custom_link" link="http://digobikas.org/gcv-biratnagar-1st-cohort/"][vc_column_text]

2nd Cohort of Green City Volunteers Biratnagar
[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Omkar Subedi
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="860" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Omkar Subedi

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Omkar Subedi is a research fellow at Digo Bikas Institute under the sustainable watch program. He is a recent bachelor's graduate in Forestry from Kathmandu Forestry College with a strong passion for climate change and sustainability and has been actively involved in various projects and initiatives related to these topics during his academic studies. Besides his educational background, he is keen on volunteering. He has been an active volunteer in Nepalese Youth for Climate Action (NYCA) as a regional coordinator for more than two years now. He believes research is the foundation for any field of study and especially in climate change, proper study and research is needed before pursuing any climate campaigns. In doing so he has prior research experience with research in climate change trends in Birendranagar, Surkhet and perception research about climate change among children alongside Save The Children. With respect to climate change, his involvement in projects related to air pollution with Clean Energy Nepal had increased his horizon in the field of environmental sustainability.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Niharika Mathema
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="854" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Niharika Mathema

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Niharika Mathema is an Architect, Placemaker and Urban Rejuvenator, specializing in Sustainable Development with a passion for People and Places. She strives to enrich people’s everyday experiences in the City by focusing on the spaces in between buildings. She holds a Master’s degree in Energy for Sustainable Social Development from Tribhuvan University - Institute of Engineering, Pulchowk/National Institute of Science and Technology, NTNU, Trondheim, Norway. Her Master's Thesis in "Sustainable Placemaking" has received high accolades. She is the founder/initiator of Placemaking Nepal, a self-organized collective of Placemakers dedicated to "Creating Meaningful Places for Memorable Experiences".  She has been leading the Placemaking conversation in Nepal and amplifying the Movement by building awareness and sensitizing people about the importance of public spaces for improved quality of life, social well-being, economic vibrancy and a healthy environment. Her research articles on pressing urban topics like Public Space Revitalization, Gender Mainstreaming, Market Cities, City Branding and Sustainable Development are published in various online portals and national dailies. She is committed to working towards a better urban future and bringing transformative changes in communities in unique ways, with a particular focus on the public realm.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: John Rai
creator: digoadmin
content: [vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="855" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]John Rai[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]John Rai serves as the Admin & Finance Officer in DBI where he oversees the financial and administrative functions of the organization, including managing the human resources department. John earned his undergraduate degree from Tribhuvan University and is currently pursuing a postgraduate degree in Masters in Business Studies at Shankar Dev Campus. With over 4 years of experience in the field of Accounts, finance, and Administration, John has a broad range of experience working in various industries such as service, banking, trading and manufacturing. He sees himself working in international finance scenarios in the future. John is passionate about dancing, traveling and exploring local cuisines outside of work.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Puja Biswas
creator: digoadmin
content: [vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="856" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]Puja Biswas[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Puja is the Province 1 focal person for DBI at the field office in Biratnagar. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Botany from Mahendra Morang Adarsha Multiple Campus and has a strong passion for social campaigns, volunteer work, and finding solutions to societal issues.  Puja has good leadership skills, having served as a coordinator of NYCA Biratnagar, received a "Best Delegate" award at the Youth International Conclave MUN 2020, and actively participated in various conferences and events across the country. Additionally, Puja is a social mobilizer for the Biratnagar Regenerative Agriculture Initiative (BiRAI) and has completed training in permaculture from Almost Heaven, as well as cabin crew and hotel management training. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Prayash Adhikari
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="852" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Prayash Adhikari

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Prayash Adhikari is a committed advocate for sustainable agriculture and environmental justice. He is doing his postgraduate in Agri-Botany and Conservation Ecology at Tribhuwan University. He has been working with youth and communities on Climate and Agriculture issues since 2015. He serves as an advisory board member for a Lamjung youth network and a national network that focuses on topics such as climate change, youth activism, and agriculture. He is the author of the Book “Gender Workload analysis In Firewood Collection: Case of Kailali, Nepal” from Lamber Academic Publications. Prayash was engaged in coordinating national and regional climate convergence events in various Nepalese cities in collaboration with the Digo Bikas Institute in 2017 and 2018. He also participated in COP 27 in Sharm el Sheikh, Egypt from DBI in 2022. He has a keen interest in climate change issues, activism, and community-based solutions, as opposed to false solutions imposed on communities on a global and local scale. He currently works as a Program Officer at the Digo Bikas Institute, where he focuses on themes such as youth involvement, agroecology, environmental justice, loss and damage, and climate research. He has also represented the Nepalese youth community on climate issues in national and international forums and presented his work and ideas on climate and agriculture challenges. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Ayush Adhikari
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="876" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Ayush Adhikari

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Aayush Adhikari is a Research Fellow in Digo Bikas Institute. He holds a Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from Tribhuvan University. His interests lie in Water Resource Management and Disaster Risk Reduction. Aayush’s prior works focus on Geotechnical Engineering, surveying, water supply systems, and noise control. Now, Aayush is working on “Energy Federalism in Nepal: Achieving Just Transition in Renewable Energy by 2050 through Municipalities.” His work explores how decentralized energy systems catalyze renewable energy transition as well as address the needs of today as well as be flexible enough to meet future demands. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Salina Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="859" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Salina Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Salina Shrestha is a research fellow at Digo Bikas Institute where she is working on the Sustainable Assessment of Asian Development Bank-supported Secondary Town Integrated Urban Environmental Improvement Project (STIUEIP). She holds a Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Engineering from Kathmandu University. She was an executive member of the Forum for Environment and Conservation Management (FECAM) and has been actively organizing and volunteering for environmental campaigns. She has gained valuable progressive experience through working and researching in multi-disciplinary disciplines such as Solid Waste, Hydrology, Environmental Modeling, Wastewater, Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA), Initial Environmental Examination (IEE),[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Go for Green: 2020 Olympics
creator: digoadmin
content: [vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/2"][vc_column_text]Digo Bikas Nepal, Jiwanta Nepal, and the Bagmati UNESCO club organized a demonstration called "Go for Green" on the eve of the Tokyo Olympics. The demonstration called on top-performing Olympic countries to reduce their carbon emissions, fulfill their climate finance obligations, and strive for real zero greenhouse gas emissions before 2050. The event was organized in collaboration with the Asian People's Movement on Debt and Development (APMDD) and featured an Olympic-themed race/hurdle race where participants leaped over hurdles with slogans such as "False Solutions," "Low Climate Targets," "Fossil Fuel Financing," and "Insufficient Climate Finance." The campaign was held in Huprachaur, Hetauda, and Madan Bhandari Smriti Park in Kathmandu, with slogans including "Fossil Fuel Free Nepal Before 2050," "Fossil Fuel Free Asia Before 2050," and "Go for Green, Go for Real Zero Emissions." Japan, the host of the 2020 Olympics, has stated its commitment to delivering a sustainable Olympics with the theme "Be better, together- for the planet and the people." The country claims to have achieved 100% renewable electricity for the Tokyo games and has made efforts to reduce CO2 emissions across Japan and around the world as a legacy of the 2020 Olympics. However, these declarations and strategies have little impact on the climate crisis as Japan is the world's fifth-largest emitter of greenhouse gases and has spent at least $10.4 billion in public finance for fossil fuel projects in recent years. The major corporate sponsors of the 2020 Tokyo Olympics include companies that invested billions of dollars in financing coal, gas, and oil projects throughout Asia and the rest of the world. Countries that have won the most medals in the history of the Olympics are mostly high-income countries that have invested billions in fossil fuel subsidies and have continued to do so during the global pandemic. To highlight the involvement of fossil fuel companies and participating countries, including Japan, that are investing in fossil fuel projects, the Asian People's Movement on Debt and Development, Asia Dirty Companies, and Asian Energy Network are calling for more serious action on the race against the climate crisis. Digo Bikas Institute is showing its solidarity with this movement and has been working to diversify Nepal's energy sources and make Nepal fossil-free by 2050. The institute has also organized Nepal Climate Convergence to capacitate youth and encourage them to engage at a local level to run the climate campaign. The campaign against fossil fuel financing needs more momentum in Nepal as the country has embarked on petroleum exploration since the memorandum of understanding between China and Nepal was signed in August 2017. Despite this, Digo Bikas Institute and Power Shift Nepal submitted a Letter of Concern to Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli in 2020 against the oil drilling project in Nepal which has a serious impact on the environment and fuels climate change. -Arati Poudel[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="1/4"][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Media Fellowship 2023
creator: digoadmin
content:
Digo Bikas Institute (DBI) together with SABAL Nepal, Saptari in collaboration with Loss and Damage Youth Coalition announces the opening of application for the Loss and Damage Media Fellowship for Saptari District on Loss and Damage 2023. The Digo Bikas Media Research Fellowship program seeks to empower early career media persons who are eager to make a difference through media engagement in the field of climate induced Loss and Damage. DBI intends to build the stories and community response on loss and damage through Media Engagement at the local level.  This year, the DBI Media Research Fellowship will grant 2 fellows (Male and Female) from Saptari district to be engaged with the community within loss and damage issues under DBI’s Research and activities performed so far. Eligibility Criteria:
  • Nepali citizen (Resident of Saptari District)
  • Prior experience in local and national media content and engagements
  • Capacity to be engaged with the community peoples and local as well as provincial government
The fellowship will be focused towards:
  • Community engagement and identification of grounded climate-induced loss and damage stories in Saptari district
  • Preparation of audio-visual media content based on the stories and community experiences
  • Publication of the media contents into local and national media networks
What does DBI Research Fellowship Cover?
  1. A grant of 10,000/- for fellowship
  2. Opportunity to share the work conducted during the fellowship with stakeholders in Saptari and in Kathmandu 
  3. A dedicated supervisor/mentor for guidance from nationally renowned media persons and supervision throughout the fellowship.
  4. Opportunity to present your findings at the local, National, and International platforms 
  5. Travel and logistic support will be provided if required for the research.
Time period: The fellows after selection will be engaged from July to August 2023 The deadline for applications is 14th July  2023, 5:00 p.m.. Please please submit your updated CV together with a Cover letter mentioning your past media works and achievements so far. to hr@digobikas.org with the subject line: Digo Bikas Media Research Fellowship 2023-Saptari Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview and final selections will be made based on a competitive evaluation process.

title: Field Officer (Biratnagar)
creator: digoadmin
content:
DBI office, Biratnagar is looking for a qualified, motivated, team player and result-oriented person to serve as Field Officer- Biratnagar for DBI works in the province. The officer will support the implementation of DBI's Program on Climate Justice and Agroecology in Koshi Province. Digo Bikas Institute invites applications from all interested and potential Nepali candidates for the following position: Interested candidates are requested to submit their application along with expected salary to hr@digobikas.org with the Title “Field Officer- Biratnagar ” along with the following documents by 19th July 2023 before 5:00 PM NST Required Documents:
  • Cover letter
  • CVs
Eligibility Criteria:
  • Nepali Citizen
  • A Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Studies, Forestry, Agriculture, Political Science, Development Studies, or related subjects is required.
  • Prior work/volunteer experience of at least 1 year.
DBI is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from women, persons living with disabilities, ethnicities, and sexual minorities. Only short-listed candidates will receive information for the further selection process. Attempting to influence at any stage of the selection process will negate the cause of the candidate. DBI reserves the right to accept or reject all or any of the applications. All the applications will be treated with the highest confidentiality.

title: Climate Justice Summer School
creator: digoadmin
content: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]The Climate Justice Summer School (CJSS) is a unique opportunity for young individuals aged 18-24 who are passionate about climate justice and eager to deepen their understanding of the climate crisis. Digo Bikas Institute is dedicated to bringing together a diverse group of young people from all over Nepal to develop a shared understanding of climate justice and explore various aspects of the climate crisis. The summer school aims to engage young individuals and provide a platform for sharing, learning, and unlearning about climate science, climate policy and politics, UN Climate negotiations, climate campaigns, research, policy analysis and real grassroots solutions to the climate crisis. The second cohort of the Climate Justice Summer School will select and engage 10-12 youth participants from across Nepal.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title="1st Cohort of Climate Justice Summer School 2022"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="1277" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_text_separator title="2nd Cohort of Climate Justice Summer School 2023"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="1284" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Omkar Subedi
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1288" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Omkar Subedi

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Omkar Subedi is a climate justice activist and the Campaign and Communication Officer at the Digo Bikas Institute. He is actively engaged in grassroots movements advocating for climate justice, with a strong focus on ensuring a just energy transition to 100% renewable energy. Omkar pushes for a rapid and equitable fossil fuel phase-out and consistently calls out false solutions that undermine real climate solutions. He is a bachelor’s graduate in Forestry from Kathmandu Forestry College with a strong passion for climate change and sustainability.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Susmita Baidawar
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1099" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Susmita Baidawar

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Susmita Baidawar is a climate justice activist and working as Climate Justice Program Officer at the Digo Bikas Institute. Currently pursuing a Master's degree in Environmental Science and Management, she is committed to advancing her knowledge on environmental issues and incorporating those knowledge into practical and real - world solutions. Susmita believes in collaboration and partnership work hence seeks opportunities to work with diverse stakeholders in creating a positive change. Motivated by a vision of a greener, sustainable, inclusive, just and equitable world, she advocates for policies and initiatives that promote environmental justice and address climate change impacts. With her passion and dedication, Susmita aims to contribute to the global fight against climate change impacts and injustices.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Rupesh Karn
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1192" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Rupesh Karn

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Rupesh Karn works as a Research Coordinator at Digo Bikas Institute (DBI). He holds bachelor´s and master’s degrees in Public Health and brings nearly four years of professional experience in health research and data management. His work spans both qualitative and quantitative methodologies. Prior to joining DBI, he held different roles at the Research and Community Development Center, Health Foundation Nepal, and the Department of Health Services. At DBI, Mr. Karn is responsible for planning, designing, and leading research initiatives. His portfolio includes reviewing, analyzing, and synthesizing research fellows’ work; developing research strategies aligned with DBI’s core principles; and facilitating capacity-building workshops for DBI staff and partner organizations.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Mandip Shah
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1191" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Mandip Shah

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Mandip Shah is the Koshi Province Coordinator at Digo Bikas Institute in Biratnagar, where he drives key initiatives focused on environmental sustainability, climate change, and community development. He is currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering from Purbanchal University, blending his technical expertise with a strong commitment to social impact in the civil society sector. With over four years of volunteer experience, Mandip's passion for climate action and environmental stewardship was sparked by his early work in various fields, which inspired him to advocate for sustainable change and address the ongoing climate crisis at the local level. As a former Network Coordinator for Nepalese Youth for Climate Action - Biratnagar, he collaborated with diverse groups, NGOs, and INGOs to promote environmental consciousness and action. Mandip is dedicated to motivating and empowering youth to take responsibility for the environment and to advocate for a green, clean, and livable future for all. His expertise in community engagement and environmental policy is evident in his efforts to create sustainable urban environments, working closely with local governments, youth, and organizations to make a lasting impact. Outside of his professional work, Mandip is deeply interested in exploring nature, conducting research, and engaging in creative endeavors. His love for nature is a driving force behind his dedication to preserving the environment for future generations.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Binisha Shrestha
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1220" img_size="medium"][vc_column_text]

Binisha Shrestha

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Binisha Shrestha is a dedicated environmental enthusiast, holds a BSc. in Environmental Science from Kathmandu University (2022). Her profound commitment to disaster resilience shines through her impactful research thesis, which focused on the "Flood Hazard Assessment of Indrawati and Tamakoshi River Basin, Nepal." Her dedication to the cause has been exemplified through her active participation in vital initiatives, such as her role as a content specialist in the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Authority's (NDRRMA) e-Learning Platform, aimed at Building NDRRMA's capacity in Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) and Gender Equality, Disability and Social Inclusion (GEDSI). During her tenure as a DRR fellow at the Youth Innovation Lab, her work was based in the challenging setting of Benighat Rorang Rural Municipality. She also took the role of Program Associate at the same organization. Beyond her professional roles, her passion for creating meaningful change is illuminated by her leadership as a Camp Leader at Action Nepal.  She has a technical skill in GIS, research, and project coordination that complements her holistic approach to disaster management, climate change adaptation and mitigation, urban migration, human geography, and geospatial technology. Her expertise has been further enriched through a diverse range of training programs and certifications. With a steadfast determination to contribute to a sustainable and resilient future, she aims to harness her skills for transformative and positive impact.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Ranjan Raj Dahal

Ranjan is an Environmental Science graduate from Tribhuvan University with over 2 years of experience in research as a research assistant at the Resources Himalaya Foundation and prior experience in Environmental Assessments as well. He has a strong dedication to climate action and activism as well. He is passionate about biodiversity conservation as well as evidence-based policy advocacy.
Equipped with a solid academic background, he has delved into research, honing skills in data analysis, experimentation, and fieldwork. Beyond academia, his volunteer engagement with Nepalese Youth in Climate Action has allowed him to effectively communicate complex ideas and inspire sustainable change in climate change advocacy at the local level in Nepal.
With a profound belief that impactful change arises from scientific insights, he is exploring the evidence-based policy advocacy approach. Biodiversity conservation is a cornerstone of his passion. He aims to explore innovative approaches to protecting Earth's intricate ecosystems and their inhabitants through research.
Transitioning forward, he is driven to lead the charge in shaping policies that harmonize human activities with the environment with a blend of academic knowledge, practical experience, and unwavering dedication. He is sure to make meaningful contributions to environmental conservation and sustainability, and advocate for a brighter future.

title: Admin and Finance Officer Job Description
creator: digoadmin
content:
DIGO BIKAS INSTITUTE (DBI)
JOB DESCRIPTION
Job Title:                               Admin and Finance Officer    Duty Station:                        DBI Office, Kathmandu Reports To:                           Program Director Staff Reporting:                    Administrative Assistant, Office Assistant   Background: Digo Bikas Institute (DBI) is a research and advocacy organization committed to promote ecological sustainability and social equity at policy and community level. Based in Kathmandu, DBI believes that Nepal (and the rest of the world) can develop meaningfully by identifying and applying alternative ways of meeting human needs without harming the nature and fellow human beings. Vision Digo Bikas Institute envisions an ecologically sound, socially just and prosperous society, and that it will continue to be so for future generations. Mission Digo Bikas Institute’s mission is to undertake research and action to stimulate informed discussion and strategic thought to advance alternative and local solutions to global  problems.   Job Purpose: This position will be overall responsible to perform the overall financial related tasks and provide support and supervision to administrative and human resources functions in the DBI Office and support area to ensure that the timely information and updates are available as required by Senior Management Team in Kathmandu and other funding partners. Key Responsibilities: Responsibility Area 1:  Finance:
  • Oversee the financial operations of Kathmandu Office and support areas to ensure timely and accurate financial reports and practices including fund management, payroll, financial disbursements, ledgers etc.
  • Preparing the vouchers of all the transactions and entering them into FAMAS Accounting Package in the concerned books of account.
  • Collect and produce period reports from all support areas and timely submission of monthly report along with the fund request of all projects to Senior Management Team and concerned funding parters complying with prevailing rules and regulation of Government, policies of the organization as well as funding partners.
  • Verify and review of financial data, supporting documents, accounting and financial reports of the each projects and recommend and implement measure to ensure that financial reports are being generated accurately and timely for the purpose of reporting different stakeholders.
  • Coordinate with the organization's internal auditor, statutory auditor and donor auditors to conduct organization audit and project audit.
  • Discuss of the findings of audit with Finance Head and Management Head in advance of finalizing audit report and incorporate audit comments.
  • Release monthly/quarterly budget to all districts as per approved DIP.
  • Analysis of expenses and payment to suppliers, Insurance Companies etc.
  • Timely deposit of TDS, PF and CIT.
  • Verify and review the transaction vouchers and supporting documents of support areas.
  • Preparation of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement.
Responsibility Area 2: Human Resource, Administration & Logistics (Supervisory Role)
  • Ensure that the office equipments and other institutional properties are well functioning and are safeguarded at all times.
  • Ensure the personal files are updated with all the required documents.
  • Ensure timely submission of the following reports to Senior Management Team, Funding Partners, and Support Areas
    1. Attendance record of all the staffs along with the leave records and other substantiating documents.
    1. Staff related document to update the personal files including the monthly time sheets.
  • Ensure that vehicles logbooks are kept accurately and up to date.
  • Ensure that the organizational properties are solely used for official purposes with proper documentation.
Responsibility Area 3:  Procurement and Store Management
  • Assist Admin Officer in procurement process (procure required goods/materials as per organization and funding partner's procurement policies, procedures and standard. of the organization ensuring their quality and cost effectiveness) and ensure the proper documentation of the procurement process.
  • Ensure safeguarding of assets and verify that assets are in existence and being used properly.
  • Ensure that logistic, procurement and store management has been done effectively following the organizational policy.
Responsibility Area 4:  Program Implementation 
  • Develop project budget in consultation with program team and management as per requirement.
  • Support in all support areas to ensure the project implementation based on the budget plan and update its expenses periodically to the Senior Management Team/Project Coordinators.
  • Ensure activities and expenditures claimed by field staffs are actual and eligible as per project documents.
  • Support program team for effective, efficiency and economically budget utilization to demonstrate the result of the concerned project.
Responsibility Area 5:  Monitoring, documentation, and reporting
  • Regularly monitor and review the field activities in each support area to ensure that activities are in line with achieving expected outputs and booked expenses complying all requirements and policy.
  • Monitor ongoing levels of expenditure on individual project outputs as budgeted and provide timely advice of likely over and under spending budget.
  • Ensure systematic documentation to prepare and submit periodic (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual and annual) financial, management and progress reports to concerned line managers as per requirements.
  • Share promptly  if found  irregularity, laps, fraud, and deviation with evidence after analyzing the finance documents.
Responsibility Area 6:  Other Tasks
  • Other responsibilities as assigned by Line Manager and Reporting Manager.
  Qualifications and Key Competencies:
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration/Business Studies with at least 2 year experience in Finance and Administration in I/NGOs.
  • Good understanding of current accounting standards, control issues/procedures, government laws and regulations and financial system of INGO/NGO.
  • Good communication, problem solving and teambuilding skills
  • Knowledge of purchasing, store management, logistic and administrative works
  • Capable of handling all types of financial logistic and administrative works. .
  • Computer skills including use of spreadsheet and electronic mail.
  • Knowledge of FAMAS (Accounting Software) will be an advantage.
  • Willingness and commitment to stay and work in remote areas of the support areas.
This job description is not incorporated into the employment contract. It is intended as a guide and should not be viewed as an inflexible specification as it may be varied from time to time in light of the organization work. The post holder will be expected to demonstrate high level of efficient and effective working skills to perform the responsibilities.

title: Jibachh Raut
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1850" img_size="large"][vc_column_text]

Jibachh Raut

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Jibachh Raut is a seasoned development professional with over 15 years of experience in grant management, finance, and support services. Since joining Digo Bikas Institute (DBI) in April 2026, he has been central to ensuring the financial integrity and operational excellence required to advance DBI’s mission of ecological sustainability and social equity. With a Master’s in Business Studies from the School of Management, Tribhuvan University, Jibachh leverages extensive expertise from global organizations—including GGGI, Swiss contact, IFAD, Goal Global, OXFAM, and ACTED—to uphold DBI's core values. He is committed to fostering transparency and accountability in all financial operations, directly supporting the institute's research and action for local solutions to global problems.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Alisha Adhikari
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1903" img_size="medium" alignment="right"][vc_column_text]

Alisha Adhikari

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text] Ms. Alisha Adhikari is an agriculture graduate from Nepal with a Master’s degree in Agricultural Economics and an early-career researcher. Her work focuses on sustainable agriculture, the water–energy–food nexus, gender equality and social inclusion (GESI), environmental sustainability, natural resource governance, and community livelihoods. She is actively engaged in youth and women’s leadership, serving as a board member of the Women Leading for Change in Natural Resource Management (WLCN). Alongside her research and leadership roles, she has contributed to youth-led organizations through volunteering and advocacy initiatives. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]

title: Swati Bhattarai
creator: digoadmin
content:
[vc_row][vc_column width="1/4"][vc_single_image image="1902" img_size="medium" alignment="center"][vc_column_text]

Swati Bhattarai

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width="3/4"][vc_column_text]Swati Bhattarai is an architect with a keen interest in art, design, and city-making. Her work explores the intersection of architecture, tactical urbanism, inclusive public spaces, and sustainable tourism. With five years of experience in urban and environmental volunteerism and advocacy through art and design, she is committed to developing innovative solutions that connect people, culture, and ecology. Her practice integrates heritage knowledge, animal rights, and the inclusion of people from diverse social backgrounds, using creative and community-driven approaches to reimagine more accessible, equitable, and ecologically sensitive urban environments.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row]
